channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Middle of the road outside the Devonshire Pub, 16th March
item.description:
These potholes are huge and dangerous. They have been there for months and have gotten to the point they are now really dangerous. The whole road surface has failed. A patch will not do. You must resurface the road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Hawley Crescent, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light out AGAIN, 16th March
item.description:
Once again this street light is out. It goes out on a regular basis. Can it be fixed so that it doesn’t keep going out please? Thank you
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Lane, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hawley Crescent falling apart, 16th March
item.description:
The number of pot holes and the severity of it is insane. This keeps getting reported on this platform and it is being ignored. This is really dangerous and negligent of the council. Shame on you. Do your job.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Hawley Crescent, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bulb out in lamp post, 16th March
item.description:
Its been out since Thursday 12th of March,its very dark on the road and pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Westergate Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Holderness House bins, 16th March
item.description:
Random broken bin left by entrance to Holderness House for over a month. One of the rubbish bins has also been stacked on the recycling bins in the communal bin area, which is dangerous and prevents access to both bins.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Estate Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous Drain, 16th March
item.description:
There is a drain at around house 57 on the street which is extremely wobbly and dangerous. I almost rolled my ankle on it badly. It's been there for around two weeks or longer.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57, Richborne Terrace, Oval, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black bags, 16th March
item.description:
Building waste

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 42, Queen's Row, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous Drain Cover, 16th March
item.description:
Drain cover damaged

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32, Marion Crescent, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flat screen TV dumped behind sign, 16th March
item.description:
Behind Allington road sign
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kilburn Lane, North Kensington, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Bench broken, 16th March
item.description:
My issue was marked as no change but please can you explain what the council are doing and why they won’t replace this bench? It is a real shame for residents who used to love sitting here. Before an election it would be amazing if Camden’s public areas were being improved.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 28a, Highgate Road, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 to 4 wider potholes near the bus stop, 16th March
item.description:
3 to 4 wider potholes near the bus stop, and it is a busy bus station and police station nearby
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Arnsberg Way, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More bags of recycling added in the street by Flat B, 16th March
item.description:
More bags added to the existing bags on the public pavement.. it’s a health & safety hazard & it attracts vermin & looks horrible!! I’ve asked them to keep it in their garden til the appropriate evening to put out your recycling.. they have ignored me .

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cranwich Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned scooter, 16th March
item.description:
Scooter has been abandoned at the end of Sidlaw House on Stamford Hill Estate. The number plate has been removed which makes me think it has been used for nefarious purposes

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Portland Avenue, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lamppost not working, 16th March
item.description:
Footbridge between 187 and 189 st Edmund’s road n9 that goes over train track. The lamppost is not working making it dark and dangerous when using footbridge.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): St Edmunds Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Household waste by the recycling bin area, 16th March
item.description:
Uncollected commercial waste outside area beside children play area

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lytham Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Van, 16th March
item.description:
The van has been parked in front of my hse since last year and the man in question who owns the van has not made any attempts to remove the van. It has broken down since last year and has not removed from the front of my address.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Daubeney Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cracked uneven payment outside 40 & 42, 16th March
item.description:
The cracked slabs have been there for years. Really need replacing I have witnessed a child tripping recently
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sundridge Avenue, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge potholes in the bike lane that needs urgent fixing, 16th March
item.description:
I reporting this problem in spring 2025 and nothing has been done. I’m fed up of the constant flat tyres when it’s too busy to cycle on the pedestrian walkway. Please can you fix the cycle lane on this stretch. The whole bit is covered in potholes and is a safety hazard for children and adults who cycle this route to school.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Anchor Iron Wharf, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loads of rubbish everywhere!!!, 16th March
item.description:
Lots of rubbish all on the pavement blocking the walkway and making it hard for disabled users to wheel their wheelchairs! Had to help an elderly person today!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Azof Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Paving slabs, 16th March
item.description:
The whole of st edmunds road n9 has very poor and dangerous pedestrian paving. It appears the soil has caused shrinkage and its caused a wave like affect on the paving slabs. I habe on many occasions slipped and am concerned about safety. It needs relaying similar that of galliard road N9.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): St Edmunds Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet
